description: Add tests covering the HOST_DATA_DIR / DATA_DIR path resolution for docker-in-docker deployments

userStories.title: Unit tests: buildDockerRunOptions uses HOST_DATA_DIR when provided
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests that verify buildDockerRunOptions receives the correct dataDir — specifically that the control plane passes HOST_DATA_DIR (not DATA_DIR) when it is set.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: apps/control-plane/src/docker-run-options.test.ts (extend existing)
Test: when only DATA_DIR is set (no HOST_DATA_DIR), volume mounts use DATA_DIR path
Test: the volume mount format is '${dataDir}/home:/home/agent', '${dataDir}/workspace:/workspace', '${dataDir}/config:/home/agent/.config'
Test: env vars HOME, XDG_CONFIG_HOME use /home/agent (not dataDir-based paths)
All existing docker-run-options tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: buildDockerRunOptions takes a 'dataDir' parameter directly — it is the caller's responsibility to pass HOST_DATA_DIR or DATA_DIR. The unit tests should verify the volume mount format given a specific dataDir input. Add two new test cases: one verifying that given dataDir='/data/tenants/abc', volumes contain '/data/tenants/abc/home:/home/agent' etc; another verifying that a DIFFERENT dataDir value (simulating HOST_DATA_DIR) produces different volume paths.

userStories.title: Integration test: control plane /start uses HOST_DATA_DIR for volume mounts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test that verifies when HOST_DATA_DIR is set and differs from DATA_DIR, the control plane uses HOST_DATA_DIR as the volume mount source for tenant containers.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: tests/integration/tc-host-data-dir.test.ts
Test sets DATA_DIR to an in-container-style path (e.g. /data/tenants) and HOST_DATA_DIR to a different host-style path (e.g. /tmp/claw-host-test)
Test calls POST /v1/tenants/:id/start (via app.inject) with a mocked docker client
Asserts that docker.run was called with volume mounts using the HOST_DATA_DIR path, NOT the DATA_DIR path
E.g. volumes should contain '/tmp/claw-host-test/<tenantId>/home:/home/agent' when HOST_DATA_DIR=/tmp/claw-host-test
Second test: when HOST_DATA_DIR is NOT set, volumes use DATA_DIR path
E.g. volumes should contain '/data/tenants/<tenantId>/home:/home/agent' when only DATA_DIR=/data/tenants
Use vi.stubEnv + vi.resetModules + dynamic import pattern for env isolation
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The relevant code is in apps/control-plane/src/app-factory.ts around line 303-315 where buildDockerRunOptions is called with hostTenantDir = (HOST_DATA_DIR ?? DATA_DIR) + '/' + tenantId. Use the mockDc pattern from tc-021.test.ts or tc-015.test.ts to capture the docker.run call and inspect its opts.volumes array. The test needs to provision a tenant first (POST /v1/tenants/provision) to create the tenant record, then call /start. Use the pattern: vi.stubEnv('DATA_DIR', '/data/tenants'); vi.stubEnv('HOST_DATA_DIR', '/tmp/claw-host-test'); vi.resetModules(); const { buildApp } = await import('../../apps/control-plane/src/app-factory.js').

userStories.description: All tests passing, committed and pushed.
